LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH BLAND'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT SETTLEMENT. COUNSEL APPEAR FOR DISCUSSION/ARGUMENT ON THE THREE ISSUES THAT WERE SET FORTH IN THE COURT'S TENTATIVE. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT, PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH BLAND'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT SETTLEMENT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 27, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. AS TO THE OVERLAP ISSUE, MS. MARASCHINO IS TO PROVIDE NOTICE TO COUNSEL IN THE RELATED PENDING ACTION: LOW V. YELP INC., NO. CGC-20-582797. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement; ***Court Reporter Fees Paid***
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. IAN SILVERS, Esq. of Bisnar Chase LLP (Tel: 949-752-2999) for Plaintiff;
Attys. MARGARET G. MARASCHINO, Esq. and DAVID W. MORESHEAD, Esq. of Munger, Tolles & Olson LLP for Defendant Yelp, Inc.

FURTHER HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH BLAND's Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear in light of written notice provided on May 20, 2021 to plaintiff's counsel in the Low v. Yelp case.

The Court notes there is no appearance at today's hearing by or for the plaintiff in the Low v. Yelp case. Counsel for parties in the present case confirm they know of no objection by or for plaintiff in the Low v. Yelp case to the present motion.

The Court rules as follows: Plaintiff's Motion for Approval of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement is granted.

Counsel for plaintiff is to submit a proposed order to the Court for review by email sent to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement; ***Court Reporter Fees Paid***
Off Calendar May-20-2021 Continued To May-27-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Ian Silvers, Esq. for Plaintiffs;
Atty. Margaret Maraschino, Esq. for Defendant Yelp, Inc.;
Atty. David Moreshead, Esq. for Defendant Yelp. Inc.

Plaintiff Elizabeth Bland's Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement. The matter is called. Counsel appear for DISCUSSION/ARGUMENT on the following three issues that were set forth in the Court's tentative:

1. The anticipated effect of the proposed settlement on the pending action in Low v. Yelp Inc., No. CGC-20-582797. (Silver Decl. 24.) In particular, is the overlapping claim for violation of Labor Code section 1174 in that action asserted on behalf of the same group of aggrieved employees and for the same time period?
2. Whether the plaintiffs/aggrieved employees in the Low action were notified of the proposed settlement and, if not, whether such notice should be provided to allow them to intervene or object.
3. Whether the proposed award of $200,000 in attorneys' fees (33 1/3 % of the gross settlement amount) should be approved in light of counsel's lodestar ($110,680).

At the conclusion of discussions/argument, Plaintiff Elizabeth Bland's Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act Settlement is CONTINUED to May 27, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302.

As to the overlap issue, Ms. Maraschino is to provide notice to counsel in the related pending action: Low v. Yelp Inc., No. CGC-20-582797 to allow time for counsel in that matter to file something with regard to Ms. Maraschino's representations made this morning or to allow for counsel in that matter to appear at the next hearing. Mr. Silvers is invited to provide a one-page declaration with the corrected figures as to the attorneys' fees prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
